author: dojo-community
name: modules
context: Organize reusable jq programs, import modules, extend library paths, and run jq tests from files
info: This skill is for jq code that has grown beyond an inline shell string. It covers filter files loaded with `-f`, module libraries stored as `.jq` files, `import` versus `include`, `-L` search paths, optional module metadata, JSON imports, and the `--run-tests` harness. Agents use this node when a jq filter should be reused across commands, committed to a repository, reviewed independently, or validated with deterministic tests.
triggers: put a jq filter in a file
body:
# jq Modules

Load this skill when the jq logic should live in version-controlled files rather than inline shell strings.

## Program Files

Use `-f` to run a jq program file directly.

~~~bash
jq -f filters/main.jq input.json
~~~

## Modules and Imports

jq modules are `.jq` files discovered through the default search path or paths added with `-L`. `import "name" as alias;` prefixes exported symbols with `alias::`, while `include "name";` brings definitions into the caller namespace.

~~~jq
module {homepage: "https://jqlang.org/manual/v1.8/"};

def normalize_name:
  tostring
  | ascii_downcase
  | gsub("[^a-z0-9]+"; "-")
  | gsub("(^-+|-+$)"; "");
~~~

~~~jq
import "slug" as slug;

map(.name |= slug::normalize_name)
~~~

## Library Paths

Use `-L` to prepend module search directories. The manual notes that module search is based on the default path or the provided `-L` path list.

~~~bash
jq -L ./jq-lib -f main.jq input.json
~~~

## Tests

`--run-tests` executes jq test files. Each test consists of the program line, one input line, one or more expected output lines, and a blank separator. Compilation failure tests start with `%%FAIL`.

~~~text
import "slug" as slug; "Hello, jq!" | slug::normalize_name
null
"hello-jq"

~~~

~~~bash
jq -L ./jq-lib --run-tests slug.tests
~~~

See `references/sample-module.jq` and `references/sample-module.tests` for a portable example bundle.

sections.title: Program files with -f
sections.body: Use `-f` when a jq program is long enough that shell quoting becomes a liability or when the filter should be committed and reviewed as its own artifact. This is the first step from one-liner to reusable jq code.

sections.title: import, include, and library paths
sections.body: `import "name" as alias;` keeps imported functions namespaced as `alias::func`, while `include "name";` behaves like textual inclusion into the caller namespace. Extend the module search path with `-L` when libraries live outside jq default locations.

sections.title: Testing jq code
sections.body: Use `jq --run-tests file.tests` to verify jq programs and modules. Each test file alternates program lines, input lines, expected output lines, and blank separators. Keep module tests near the module and use `-L` when libraries live in a sibling directory.

sections.title: Module metadata
sections.body: The optional `module { ... };` directive attaches metadata that can be read with `modulemeta`. jq itself does not require it for execution, but metadata is useful when modules should describe their homepage, search hints, or exported capabilities.

scripts.name: Run Program File
scripts.description: Execute a jq program from a file with optional library paths and named variables

JQ_PROGRAM_FILE.description: Path to the jq program file to execute with -f

JQ_INPUT.description: Inline JSON or raw text input

JQ_NULL_INPUT.description: Use -n and do not read an input file or stdin

JQ_RAW_INPUT.description: Treat input as raw text via -R

JQ_SLURP.description: Read all inputs into one array or one raw string via -s

JQ_RAW_OUTPUT.description: Write strings without JSON quotes via -r

JQ_LIBRARY_PATH.description: Colon-separated list of additional jq library directories

JQ_VARS_JSON.description: JSON object whose keys become jq variables through --argjson

scripts.name: Run jq Tests
scripts.description: Execute a jq test file with optional library paths

JQ_TEST_FILE.description: Path to the jq test file

JQ_LIBRARY_PATH.description: Colon-separated list of additional jq library directories

programFile.description: jq program file path for run-file

input.description: Inline JSON or raw text input for run-file

testFile.description: jq test file path for run-tests

stdout.description: Program or test output from jq

depends.reason: Reusable jq files depend on from-file, library-path, and variable flags
The packaged wrapper scripts run in Bash
Program files, modules, and test fixtures are file-based assets
